#[derive(Default)]
pub struct Options {}

impl Options {
    /// Creates new [`Options`] instance with the required fields.
    pub const fn new() -> Self {
        Self {}
    }

    /// Registers a misc device using the configured options.
    pub fn register(
        &self,
        reg: Pin<&mut Registration>,
        name: fmt::Arguments<'_>,
    ) -> Result {
        reg.register_with_options(name, self)
    }

    /// Allocates a new registration of a misc device and completes the registration with the
    /// configured options.
    pub fn register_new(
        &self,
        name: fmt::Arguments<'_>,
    ) -> Result<Pin<Box<Registration>>> {
        let mut r = Pin::from(Box::try_new(Registration::new())?);
        self.register(r.as_mut(), name)?;
        Ok(r)
    }
}

pub struct Registration {
    registered: bool,
    tty_driver: bindings::tty_driver,
    name: Option<CString>,
    _pin: PhantomPinned,
}

impl Registration {
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            registered: false,
            tty_driver:bindings::tty_driver::default(),
            name: None,
            _pin: PhantomPinned,
        }
    }

    pub fn new_pinned(name: fmt::Arguments<'_>) -> Result<Pin<Box<Self>>> {
        Options::new().register_new(name)
    }

    pub fn register(
        self: Pin<&mut Self>,
        name: fmt::Arguments<'_>,
    ) -> Result {
        Options::new().register(self, name)
    }

    pub fn register_with_options(
        self:Pin<&mut Self>,
        name: fmt::Arguments<'_>,
        opts: &Options,
    )->Result{
        // SAFETY: We must ensure that we never move out of `this`.
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        if this.registered {
            // Already registered.
            return Err(EINVAL);
        }

        let name = CString::try_from_fmt(name)?;
        //self.owner = module_ptr;
        this.tty_driver.driver_name = "tty0tty".as_ptr() as *const _;
        this.tty_driver.name = "tnt".as_ptr() as *const _;
        this.tty_driver.major = TTY0TTY_MAJOR as i32;
        this.tty_driver.minor_start = TTY0TTY_MINOR as i32;
        this.tty_driver.type_ = bindings::TTY_DRIVER_TYPE_SERIAL as i16;
        this.tty_driver.subtype = bindings::SERIAL_TYPE_NORMAL as i16;
        this.tty_driver.flags =
        (bindings::TTY_DRIVER_RESET_TERMIOS | bindings::TTY_DRIVER_REAL_RAW) as u64;
        this.tty_driver.init_termios = unsafe { bindings::tty_std_termios };
        this.tty_driver.init_termios.c_iflag = 0;
        this.tty_driver.init_termios.c_oflag = 0;
        this.tty_driver.init_termios.c_cflag = bindings::B38400 | bindings::CS8 | bindings::CREAD;
        this.tty_driver.init_termios.c_lflag = 0;
        this.tty_driver.init_termios.c_ispeed = 38400;
        this.tty_driver.init_termios.c_ospeed = 38400;

        this.registered = true;

        let retval = unsafe { bindings::tty_register_driver(&mut this.tty_driver) };
        if retval != 0 {
            unsafe { bindings::tty_driver_kref_put(&mut this.tty_driver) };
            return Err(Error::from_kernel_errno(retval));
        }

        this.name = Some(name);

        Ok(())
    }

}

impl Default for Registration {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self::new()
    }
}

unsafe impl Sync for Registration {}

#[allow(clippy::non_send_fields_in_send_ty)]

use crate::{
    bindings, device, error::code::*, error::from_kernel_result, str::CStr, to_result,

use alloc::boxed::Box;
use bindings::tty_driver;
use core::{
    cell::UnsafeCell,
    marker::{PhantomData, PhantomPinned},
    pin::Pin,
    ptr, fmt
};

use macros::vtable;

const TTY0TTY_MAJOR: usize = 240;  // experimental range
const TTY0TTY_MINOR: usize = 16;

pub trait TtyMethods {}

pub struct Registration {
    this_module: &'static crate::ThisModule,
    registered: bool,
    tty_driver: bindings::tty_driver,
    name: &'static CStr,
    _p: PhantomPinned,
}

impl Registration {
    pub fn new(this_module: &'static crate::ThisModule, name: &'static CStr) -> Self {
        Self {
            this_module,
            registered: false,
            tty_driver: bindings::tty_driver::default(),
            name,
            _p: PhantomPinned,
        }
    }

    pub fn new_pinned(
        this_module: &'static crate::ThisModule,
        name: &'static CStr
    ) -> Result<Pin<Box<Self>>> {
        let mut registration = Pin::from(Box::try_new(Self::new(this_module, name))?);
        registration.as_mut().register()?;

        Ok(registration)
    }

    pub fn register(self: Pin<&mut Self>,) -> Result {
        // SAFETY: We must ensure that we never move out of `this`.
        let this = unsafe { self.get_unchecked_mut() };
        if this.registered {
            pr_err!("Driver already registered\n");
            // Already registered.
            return Err(EINVAL);
        }

        let mut tty_driver = this.tty_driver;

        pr_info!("Registering tty driver\n");

        // set owner field
        tty_driver.owner = this.this_module as *const _ as *mut bindings::module;

        tty_driver.driver_name = this.name.as_ptr() as *const core::ffi::c_char;
        tty_driver.name = "tnt".as_ptr() as *const _;
        tty_driver.major = TTY0TTY_MAJOR as i32;
        tty_driver.minor_start = TTY0TTY_MINOR as i32;
        tty_driver.type_ = bindings::TTY_DRIVER_TYPE_SERIAL as i16;
        tty_driver.subtype = bindings::SERIAL_TYPE_NORMAL as i16;
        tty_driver.flags = (bindings::TTY_DRIVER_RESET_TERMIOS | bindings::TTY_DRIVER_REAL_RAW) as u64;
        tty_driver.init_termios = unsafe { bindings::tty_std_termios };
        tty_driver.init_termios.c_iflag = 0;
        tty_driver.init_termios.c_oflag = 0;
        tty_driver.init_termios.c_cflag = bindings::B38400 | bindings::CS8 | bindings::CREAD;
        tty_driver.init_termios.c_lflag = 0;
        tty_driver.init_termios.c_ispeed = 38400;
        tty_driver.init_termios.c_ospeed = 38400;



        pr_info!("---before res---\n");
        let res = unsafe { bindings::tty_register_driver(&mut tty_driver) };
        pr_info!("---after res---\n");
        if res != 0 {
            this.registered = false;
            pr_err!("Failed to register tty driver, error code: {}\n", res);
            unsafe { bindings::tty_driver_kref_put(&mut tty_driver) };
            return Err(Error::from_kernel_errno(res));
        }
        pr_info!("Successfully registered tty driver\n");
        this.registered = true;

        Ok(())
    }
}

impl Drop for Registration {
    fn drop(&mut self) {
        // SAFETY: We are simply unregistering an `spi_driver` that we know to be valid.
        // [`DriverRegistration`] instances can only be created by being registered at the
        // same time, so we are sure that we'll never unregister an unregistered `spi_driver`.
        unsafe { bindings::tty_unregister_driver(&mut self.tty_driver) }
    }
}

it seems the alloc is success but still "bindings::tty_register_driver(tty_driver)" caused panic

I carefully examined the implementation of the tty_register_driver function in the original C module. Through methods like logging, I identified that the issue was with the line proc_tty_register_driver(driver);. Upon further inspection of the proc implementation, I found the following check:

if (!driver->driver_name || driver->proc_entry || !driver->ops->proc_show)
    return;

Here, I noticed it checks for driver->ops->proc_show, but in my previous implementation, I hadn't set the driver->ops field, which caused a kernel panic due to failing this check. To resolve this, I proceeded to implement this part and set the driver->ops field in an unsafe manner. Now, the kernel runs without crashing. Here is my current implementation:

// SPDX-License-Identifier: GPL-2.0
//! Tty0tty test

use core::{f32::consts::E, ops::DerefMut,ptr,mem};
use kernel::{
    bit,  device, bindings, tty::*,
    file::{File, Operations},
    sync::{smutex::Mutex, Arc},
    power, miscdev, Module,
    prelude::*,
};
use kernel::c_str;
use kernel::io_buffer::{IoBufferReader, IoBufferWriter};

const DRIVER_VERSION: &str = "v1.2";
const DRIVER_AUTHOR: &str = "Wenzhaoliao";
const DRIVER_DESC: &str = "tty0tty null modem driver";

// out
const MCR_DTR: usize = 0x01;
const MCR_RTS: usize = 0x02;
const MCR_LOOP: usize = 0x04;

// in
const MSR_CTS: usize = 0x10;
const MSR_CD: usize = 0x20;
const MSR_DSR: usize = 0x40;
const MSR_RI: usize = 0x80;

struct TTY0TTYMethods;

struct Tty0tty {
    _tty: *mut bindings::tty_driver,
}

static mut SERIAL_OPS: bindings::tty_operations = bindings::tty_operations {
    open: None,
    close: None,
    write: None,
    write_room: None,
    ioctl: None,
    lookup: None,
    install: None,
    remove: None,
    shutdown: None,
    cleanup: None,
    put_char: None,
    flush_chars: None,
    chars_in_buffer: None,
    compat_ioctl: None,
    set_termios:None,
    throttle: None,
    unthrottle: None,
    stop: None,
    start: None,
    hangup: None,
    break_ctl: None,
    flush_buffer: None,
    set_ldisc: None,
    wait_until_sent: None,
    send_xchar: None,
    tiocmget: None,
    tiocmset: None,
    resize: None,
    get_icount: None,
    get_serial: None,
    set_serial: None,
    show_fdinfo: None,
    proc_show: None,
};

static mut TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER: *mut bindings::tty_driver = ptr::null_mut();

unsafe impl Sync for Tty0tty {}

impl kernel::Module for Tty0tty {
    fn init(_name: &'static CStr, mut _module: &'static ThisModule) -> Result<Self> {

         // 模仿C代码中的打印信息
         pr_info!("--------------------------\n");
         pr_info!("tty0tty init\n");
         pr_info!("--------------------------\n");

         // read pairs，cal pair_count
         let lock = _module.kernel_param_lock();
         let pair_count = 2 * pairs.read(&lock);


         let module_ptr = _module.as_ptr();

         unsafe { TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER =  bindings::__tty_alloc_driver(pair_count as u32, module_ptr, 0)  ;
        if  unsafe {bindings::IS_ERR(TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER as *const core::ffi::c_void)}  {
            let err = unsafe { bindings::PTR_ERR(TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER as *const core::ffi::c_void) };
            pr_info!("alloc failed: {}\n", err);
            return Err(kernel::Error::from_kernel_errno(err.try_into().unwrap()));
        }
        else {
            pr_info!("alloc successed with capability:{}",pair_count);
        }
            // 初始化tty driver
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).owner = module_ptr;
            pr_info!("assign owner success\n");
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).driver_name = c_str!("tty0tty").as_ptr() as *const i8;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).name = c_str!("tnt").as_ptr() as *const i8;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).major = 240;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).minor_start = 16;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).type_ = bindings::TTY_DRIVER_TYPE_SERIAL as i16;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).subtype = bindings::SERIAL_TYPE_NORMAL as i16;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).flags = (bindings::TTY_DRIVER_RESET_TERMIOS | bindings::TTY_DRIVER_REAL_RAW) as u64;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios = bindings::tty_std_termios;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios.c_iflag = 0;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios.c_oflag = 0;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios.c_cflag = bindings::B38400 | bindings::CS8 | bindings::CREAD;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios.c_lflag = 0;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios.c_ispeed = 38400;
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).init_termios.c_ospeed = 38400;

            // 直接设置 ops 字段
            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).ops = &SERIAL_OPS;
        }

         // 注册tty driver
         pr_info!("start register\n");
         let retval = unsafe { bindings::tty_register_driver(TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER) };
         if retval != 0 {
             pr_err!("failed to register tty0tty tty driver");
             unsafe {
                 bindings::tty_driver_kref_put(TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER);
             }
             return Err(Error::from_kernel_errno(retval));
         }
         pr_info!("end register\n");

         pr_info!("tty driver registration created\n");

         Ok(Tty0tty { _tty: unsafe { TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER } })
     }
}

                    mcr = (*other_tty).mcr;
                }
            }

        if (mcr & MCR_RTS) == MCR_RTS {
            msr |= MSR_CTS;
        }

        if (mcr & MCR_DTR) == MCR_DTR {
            msr |= MSR_DSR | MSR_CD;
        }

        (*tty).driver_data = tty0tty as *mut c_void;
        (*tty0tty).tty = tty;

fn do_close(tty0tty: *mut Tty0ttySerial) {
    unsafe {
        if tty0tty.is_null() || (*tty0tty).tty.is_null() {
            return;
        }

        let mut msr = 0;
        let index = (*(*tty0tty).tty).index;
        if index % 2 == 0 {

            if !TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1).is_null() {
                let other_tty = &mut **TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize + 1);
                (*other_tty).msr = msr;

        } else {

            if !TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize - 1).is_null() {
                let other_tty = &mut **TTY0TTY_TABLE.offset(index as isize - 1);
                (*other_tty).msr = msr;
            }
        }

        if (*tty0tty).open_count == 0{
            return;
        }
        else{
            (*tty0tty).open_count-=1;
        }
    }

            (*TTY0TTY_TTY_DRIVER).subtype = bindings::SERIAL_TYPE_NORMAL as i16;
